name: Force Majeure Event
description: An extraordinary event has occurred that may excuse performance under the contract. Simulate whether the event qualifies as force majeure and what contractual consequences follow.

parameters.name: event_duration_days
parameters.description: Expected duration of the event in days

parameters.name: impact_description
parameters.description: How the event impacts the ability to perform
parameters.default_value: Unable to deliver services due to government-mandated facility closures

analysis_guidance: Focus on: (1) Force majeure definition — does the contract define force majeure? Does this event fall within the definition? (2) Notice requirements — what notice must the affected party provide? Within what timeframe? (3) Mitigation obligations — is the affected party required to mitigate? What steps? (4) Performance suspension — is performance suspended or excused? For how long? (5) Termination trigger — if the force majeure event persists beyond a threshold, can either party terminate? (6) Financial allocation — who bears the financial burden during force majeure? (7) Alternative performance — does the contract require alternative means of performance? (8) Insurance — does the contract require force majeure or business interruption insurance? (9) MAC/MAE clauses — is there a material adverse change clause that interacts with force majeure?
